task: Implement a JavaScript function `normalizePath(path)` that normalizes a Unix-style path string the way Python `os.path.normpath` does, returning the normalized path string. Rules: (1) Collapse any run of repeated slashes into a single slash. (2) Drop every `.` (current-directory) component. (3) Resolve each `..` by popping the previous REAL component. (4) For an ABSOLUTE path (leading `/`), the result keeps its single leading `/`, and a `..` that would escape the root is simply dropped (`/..` -> `/`, `/a/../..` -> `/`). (5) For a RELATIVE path, leading `..` components that cannot be popped are KEPT (`a/b/../../..` -> `..`, `../x` -> `../x`), and a leading `./` is removed (`./a` -> `a`). (6) The empty string normalizes to `.`, a lone `.` stays `.`, and a trailing slash is removed except for the root itself. (7) Do NOT special-case a leading `//`: collapse it to a single `/` (`//a//b/` -> `/a/b`, `//` -> `/`). Worked examples (input -> expected): `/a/./b/../c` -> `/a/c`; `/../` -> `/`; `../../model-benchmark` -> `..`; `//a//b/` -> `/a/b`; `` (empty) -> `.`; `/` -> `/`; `a/./b` -> `a/b`; `/a/b/c/../../` -> `/a`; `x/../../y` -> `../y`. These are adversarial cases: `..` popping past the root on an absolute path (dropped) versus kept on a relative path, repeated-slash collapse including a leading `//`, and the empty/`.`/trailing-slash edge cases — exactly where a naive split-join, a path that mishandles `..` at the root, or one that special-cases `//` diverges. Return ONLY the function source as text. Do NOT create or write any files.
visibleSpec: normalizePath(path) -> string. Normalizes a Unix-style path like os.path.normpath: collapse repeated slashes, drop `.` components, resolve `..` by popping the previous real component. Absolute paths keep a leading `/` and drop `..` at the root; relative paths keep un-poppable leading `..`. Empty string -> `.`; `.` -> `.`; trailing slash removed except for root; a leading `//` collapses to `/` (not special-cased).
scope: Single pure function returning a string. No mutation of the input, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries, no use of the Node path module. Input is a Unix-style path string.

hidden_tests.name: relative pop to empty is dot
